publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-13 00:16:43 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The Patricia Morrisroe papers were created and assembled in the process of writing her biography of Robert Mapplethorpe. They include original interview tapes that she recorded, interview transcripts, correspondence, research files, notebooks, catalogues of most of Mapplethorpe's exhibitions, publishing and movie contracts, communications, book reviews, and more.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Materials created and assembled in the process of Patricia Morrisroe's writing of her biography of Robert Mapplethorpe, including the original, unexpurgated interview tapes that she recorded from 1988 to 1990, including those of Mapplethorpe, his family, school friends, his priest, major collaborators, models, lovers and curators, and gallery owners. The collection also includes correspondence, interview transcripts, including many done by phone with Patti Smith, research files, notebooks, invitations and catalogues of most of Mapplethorpe's exhibitions, original Random House and foreign contracts for the book, an Advance Reader's Copy marked up by Random House publisher and president Harry Evans, approximately 50 faxes to Morrisroe's agent, Kathy Robbins, movie contracts, approximately 200 reviews of the book, and 29 carbon copies of letters written to Mapplethorpe by Milton Moore, the love his life and the subject of his most famous photograph – "The Man in Polyester Suit."

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Patricia Morrisroe is an American journalist and author. She has written three non-fiction books, a novel, and her journalism has appeared in the New York Times, New York, Vanity Fair and the Sunday Times Magazine (London).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Patricia Morrisroe papers, #8805. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 2 restricted until 2035. Access to audio visual materials is restricted until digitization is complete. Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: From Series I. (Books):
Lyon, L. (1983). Robert Mapplethorpe. New York: The Viking Press.
Morrisroe, P. (1995). Mapplethorpe: a biography. New York: Random House (inscribed by author).
From Series IV, subseries IVa. (Books):
Celant, G., ed., Mapplethorpe, R. and Städtische Kunsthalle Düsseldorf. (1992). Mapplethorpe versus Rodin. Milano: Electa.
Frankfurter Kunstverein and Weiermair, P. (1981). Robert Mapplethorpe. Frankfurt: Frankfurter Kunstverein.

Kardon J., Joselit D., Larson K., and the University of Pennsylvania. (1990). Robert Mapplethorpe : the perfect moment (3rd ed.). Philadelphia: University of Pennsylvania Institute of Contemporary Art.

Mapplethorpe R., Christie, Manson, and Woods, and Robert Mapplethorpe Foundation. (1989). The collection of Robert Mapplethorpe : sold for the benefit of the Robert Mapplethorpe Foundation. New York: Christie's.
Mapplethorpe R. and National Portrait Gallery (Great Britain). (1988). Mapplethorpe portraits. London: National Portrait Gallery.
Mapplethorpe R. (1987). Robert Mapplethorpe. Tokyo: Parco Vision Contemporary.
Mapplethorpe, R. (1993) Robert Mapplethorpe 1992 – 1993. New York/Tokyo: Robert Mapplethorpe Foundation/Tokyo Metropolitan Teien Art Museum.
Mapplethorpe R. and Shange, N. (1986). Robert Mapplethorpe black book (1st ed.). New York: St. Martin's Press.
Marshall, R. and Mapplethorpe, R. (1986). 50 New York artists : a critical selection of painters and sculptors working in New York. San Francisco: Chronicle Books.
From Series IV, subseries IVb. (Books):
Bockris. V. and Bayler, R. (1999) Patti Smith. New York: Simon and Schuster.
Bromberg, C. (1989). The Wicked Ways of Malcolm McLaren. New York: Harper and Row (with Morrisroe's highlighting and underlining).
Burchill, J. and Parsons, T. (1978). The Boy Looked at Johnny: The Obituary of Rock and Roll. London: Pluto Press (with Morrisroe's highlighting and underlining).
Carroll, J. (1987). The Basketball Diaries. New York: Penguin Books.
Carroll, J. (1987). Forced Entries: The Downtown Diaries: 1971-1973. New York: Penguin Books.
Turner, F. (1987). At the Chelsea. San Diego: Harcourt Brace Jovanovich (with Morrisroe's highlighting and underlining).

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: His Life And Times. A Book Proposal by Patricia Morrisroe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon typescript. Clip bound. 49 leaves, 49 pages. Includes Morrisroe's introduction and sample chapters. Written for book sale in the UK.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated carbon typescript. Approximately 200 leaves. Annotations and comments throughout by Random House/Turtle Bay Books editor Susan Kamil.

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Complete early draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated carbon typescript. 496 leaves, 496 pages. Annotations, corrections and comments throughout by Random House editor Kate Medina.

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Setting copy. "Kate's edits"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heavily annotated carbon typescript. 581 leaves, 581 pages. Edits and corrections by Kate Medina.

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Setting copy--annotated and incomplete
Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Bound first galley. "Uncorrected Proof"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blue, softcover, wraps. Random House. 435 leaves, 435 pages. Contains four annotated corrections by Morrisroe.

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Bound, heavily revised, galley. "Last minute changes in galley form- per Harry Evans".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blue, softcover, wraps. Random House. 435 leaves, 435 pages. Heavily annotated copy containing Harry Evans' tipped in revisions and changes. Includes three, staple bound, pages of "Comments by Harold Evans".

did.unittitle: Mapplethorpe: A Biography. Patricia Morrisroe. Dust jacket mock-up
Series II. Correspondence, contracts, and legal matters
Subseries IIa. Correpondence and legal matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. TLS. Stephen Koch to Patricia Morrisroe. March 17, 1991. 1 leaf, 1 page. 2. TLS. Gerard Malanga to Patricia Morrisroe. August 8, 1991. 1 leaf, 1 page. 2. TLS. Edward de Celle to Patricia Morrisroe. August 18, 1989. 1 leaf, 1 page. 3. TLS. Jeffrey Fraenkel to Patricia Morrisroe. November 3, 1989. 1 leaf, 1 page. 4. TLS. Allen Ellenzweig to Patricia Morrisroe. August 14, 1990. 1 leaf, 1 page. 5. ALS. Pierre Apraxine to Patricia Morrisroe. Undated ("Thursday 20th"). 1 leaf, 1 page. 6. TLS. Jack Fritscher to Patricia Morrisroe. March 7, 1990. 2 leaves, 2 pages. 7. ALS. Patti Smith to Patricia Morrisroe. Undated (ca. Nov 1989). 1 leaf, 1 page. 8. [5] ALS. Jesse Turner. ca. 1989. Approx. 15 pages total. Five autograph letters from Turner, who lived with Mapplethorpe and Smith in the Chelsea in the early 1970s. 9. TLS. Paul Schmidt to Robert Mapplethorpe. March 21, 1983. 3 pages, 3 leaves. Includes poem Schmidt dedicated to Mapplethorpe. 10. ALS. Harold Evans to Patricia Morrisroe. May 17, 1995. 1 leaf. 11. ALS. Harold Evans to Patricia Morrisroe. Jan. 4, 1995. 2 pages, 2 leaves. 12. ALS. Kate Medina to Patricia Morrisroe. Undated. 1 leaf 13. ALS. James Gavin to Patricia Morrisroe. Oct. 15, 1995. 1 card 14. Fax. Patricia Morrisroe to Elizabeth Mackey. including marked up book flap copy 15. Fax. Patricia Morrisroe to Kathy Robbins. Jan. 18, 1995 1 page. (one page missing.) 16. Fax. Patricia Morrisroe to Kate Medina. Aug. 30, 1994. 1 page. 17. Fax. Patricia Morrisroe to Kate Medina. Oct. 4, 1995. 1 page. 18. Fax. (Undated) Patricia Morrisroe to Kathy Robbins. 19. Fax. Patricia Morrisroe to Kathy Robbins. May 18, 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of (29) autograph letters sent by Milton Moore to Mapplethorpe between 1982 – 1988. Moore, who would become the love of Mappelethorpe's life, was also one of his most well-known models and muses, often referred to as "The Man in the Polyester Suit". After their relationship permanently ended, Moore, a diagnosed schizophrenic, would be convicted of murder and sent to prison. These letters span the years of Moore and Mapplethorpe's first serious break-up, their numerous reconciliations, and the photographers rise to worldwide acclaim. All are hand written and, on average, 2 – 4 pages in length. They are of a close, personal, nature, dealing with everything from the minutiae of their shared time together (the sharing of bills) to the dissolution of their relationship (the returning of personal property) to heartfelt confessions of love, bitterness and longing. These letters speak directly to the complex, conflicted, nature of Moore's feelings for Mapplethorpe, while hinting at the Machiavellian machinations behind their relationship.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. "Last Will and Testament of Robert Mapplethorpe". Annotated carbon typescript. 14 leaves, 14 pages. Amended and finalized on June 23, 1988. 2. Sam Wagstaff. Clipbound carbon copies of 35 invoices from the Rinhart Galleries of photographs purchased by Wagstaff ca. 1973 – 1980. 3. CLS. Robert Mapplethorpe. Mapplethorpe letter on Mapplethorpe letterhead, dated November 10, 1988, acknowledging Patricia Morrisroe as his authorized biographer. 4. Report from Ned Rosenthal, Random House Law Department, on potentially litigious passages in the biography. August 4, 1994. Combination carbon typescript and handwritten pages. 13 leaves, 13 pages. 5. ALS. Lesley Oelsner to Patricia Morrisroe. August 10, 1992. With attached letter to Jack Fritscher from Patricia Morrisroe 6. ALS. Jack Fritscher to Patricia Morrisroe. June 24, 1992. 6 leaves 7. Patricia Morrisroe's responses to Random House legal review 8. Macmillan legal review and Patricia Morrisroe's responses to editor Georgia Garrett 9. ALS. Retainer and fee agreement from Ned Rosenthal re: Alex Knaust subpoena, 2 leaves. Dec. 23, 1991 10. ALS. Three letters from Ned Rosenthal, including 6 invoices for services rendered from Dec. 1991 to July 1992.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIb. Contracts, movie option, and photo rights

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes original letter from Robert Mapplethorpe on Mapplethorpe stationary (dated Nov. 10, 1988) authorizing Morrisroe as his biographer; original Random House contracts; correspondence between Kathy Robbins, Morrisroe's agent, and Carol Judy Leslie, Mapplethorpe's agent, setting forth the terms of the agreement between Morrisroe and Mapplethorpe; memorandums of payment from Random House;letter from Mapplethorpe's lawyer, Michael Ward Stout, stating Stout's decision not to enter into a participation agreement with Morrisroe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes material related to Mapplethorpe photo rights and other permissions; letter between original editor, Susan Kamil, and Mapplethorpe agent, Carol Leslie confirming issues pertaining to the Mapplethorpe pictures in the book; dozens of faxes from Morrisroe to Kathy Robbins detailing issues with Mapplethorpe pictures; numerous faxes to Kate Medina, Random House editor, detailing issues gaining access to photographs; faxes to Kathy Robbins related to Patti Smith; letters from Art & Commerce related to Random House's payment for Mapplethorpe pictures; faxes from Morrisroe to Vincent Virga, freelance photo editor hired by Random House to handle the pictures; faxes to Kathy Robbins related to difficulty securing photo rights for foreign editions; letter from John Charles Thomas, Michael Stout's law partner, threatening legal action; draft letter from Robbin's lawyer disputing contents of Thomas letter; letter from photo editor Vincent Virga to Kathy Robbins about his understanding that he was securing world rights; invoices from Art & Commerce re: the usage of Mapplethorpe pictures; copies of non-Mapplethorpe pictures that were used in the book; notes from a 2019 conversation with Kathy Robbins's assistant about Art & Commerce's decision not to grant the Korean publisher Euyloo the rights to license Mapplethorpe pictures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes contracts for foreign editions, including Macmillan catalogue and correspondence. ALS. Georgia Garrett to Patricia Morrisroe. Aug 4, 1994. 1 leaf; ALS from Roland Phillips to Patricia Morrisroe. ALS from Michael Dorr to Patricia Morrisroe, July 26, 1996. Copies of German contract; Japanese contract; Brazilian contract; Dutch contract.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes Random House generated book publicity; Harry M. Evans' (Random House Publisher) invitation to an event (with Patti Smith) at the Chelsea Piers commemorating the anniversary of the Corcoran Gallery canceling Mapplethorpe's "Perfect Moment" exhibition; Morrisroe's interview itinerary; Morrisroe's book tour itinerary; excerpt from Mapplethorpe included in "Random Lives." Turtle Bay catalogue listing Mapplethorpe (with picture of original cover) as a March, 1993 release. (Turtle Bay, an imprint of Random House, was the original publisher), story on Morrisroe in "At Random"; press on the cancellation of Harry Evans' event due to Michael Stout's refusal to allow him to use Mapplethorpe's photographs; ads for book in New York Times; Morrisroe's notes on index cards related to talks to she gave on book tour.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes material related to movie option of Mapplethorpe; contract with Universal/Longview Entertainment; memorandum of payment; articles related to option; mark up of contract with Katja Pictures/New Line Productions; letters from director Tom Kalin expressing interest in directing movie with Christine Vachon as producer; copy of email to Mona Card, one of the producers of HBO Mapplethorpe documentary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing press clips; approximately 200 reviews, articles and essays related to the book, from small-town newspapers to the New York Times, the New Yorker, New York Review of Books; profiles of Morrisroe in New York Times, San Francisco Examiner, San Francisco Chronicle, New York Newsday and W magazine. Copy of Vanity Fair with book excerpt, "Demon Romantics."

did.unittitle: Series III. Interview cassettes and transcripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Mort #4" -- B-side label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tape 1 (tr-20030) includes name "Wm. Targ" on label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tape 1 (tr-20030) includes name "Wm. Targ" on label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Nov. 16, Nov. 17" -- original label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Nov. 16, Nov. 17" -- original label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Mort #4" -- B-side label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Mort #4" -- B-side label.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included in the folder is a photocopy of an invitation to Wagstaff's opening on March 29, 1979 at the Montgomery Art Gallery (Pomona College); a broadside for a Wagstaff exhibition at the dell'International Center of Photography in Italy in 1979; and the NYU press release dated 6/1/78 for the first major exhibition of photographs from the Sam Wagstaff Collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annotated carbon copies of correspondence between Mapplethorpe and Sam Wagstaff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 25 invitations to Mapplethorpe openings ca. 1978 – 1988. In addition, folder contains flyers and press releases for premiere screenings of Robert Mapplethorpe's short film "Still Moving/Patti Smith" ca. March/ April 1978.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains annotated and highlighted carbon copies of interviews with Mapplethorpe from the 1980s. In addition, present in this folder are Morrisroe's handwritten notes regarding a Spanish documentary about Mapplethorpe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Highlighted and annotated carbon reviews of Mapplethorpe's openings in the 1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Morrisroe's hand written research and interview notes. Includes memo notebooks and dozens of loose leaves. Notes center around all of the major voices/persons in the book including Sam Wagstaff, John McKendry, Mapplethorpe's college roommate, etc.

did.unittitle: Subseries IVb. Clippings and research files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Mapplethrope to de la Falaise's husband, John McKendry, Curator of Prints and Photographs at the Metropolitan Museum of Art from 1967-1975.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes numerous annotated articles on Patti Smith. Also includes an Arista Records Patti Smith publicity still; signed first edition of Smith's Ha! Ha! Houdini [1977]; issue # 2 of PUNK magazine; 8/5/82 birth announcement of Jackson Frederick Smith, son of Fred "Sonic" Smith and Patti Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes numerous, annotated, articles on Smith in addition to invitation to Smith's "Rock n' Rimbaud" reading [October 20, 1977 at Veith Turske Gallery]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes numerous annotated articles on Patti Smith surrounding the release of her Wave LP.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder Includes numerous annotated articles and clippings on Patti Smith surrounding the release of her Dream of Life LP.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles on Sam Shepard and the Chelsea hotel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles on NYC clubs and club culture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In addition to articles and clippings on AIDS, folder includes invitation to Morrisroe to an AIDS benefit he was hosting on January 17, 1989, and a letter to Mappletorpe from John Lilly re: a new AIDS therapy dated February 6, 1989.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation to Mapplethorpe memorial at the Whitney Museum of American Art on May 22, 1989, two copies of memorial card w/ poem by Patti Smith, and Morrisroe's notes on service written on the back of program.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes polaroids of Mapplethorpe's apartment, and Morrisroe's diary of her meetings with him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. "Notes from Card Catalogue and Date Book 1986 – 1988". National Brand. Spiral bound. 10 X 8. 80 leaves, 80 pages. Includes Morrisroe's notes on Mapplethorpe's shooting diary 1986 – 1987. Also, Morrisroe's notes on Mapplethorpe's writings/drawings the day before he passed away ca. 1989. 2. [47] spiral bound notebooks and legal pads containing Morrisroe's notes. These notebooks are predominantly interview notebooks, many of which have been transcribed (see boxes 9-11). In addition, notebooks include those on Mapplethorpe's loft, artist Jack Walls, photographer Lynn Davis, notes on photographs and Mapplethorpe's commercial work.

did.unittitle: Series V. Books and exhibition catalogs
Subseries Va. Mapplethorpe printed matter and exhibition catalogs
Robert Mapplethorpe: Photographs. Chrysler Museum at Norfolk
Robert Mapplethorpe. Rein von der Fuhr, ed. Galerie Jurka: Amsterdam
Intimate Architecture: Contemporary Clothing Design. Susan Sidlauskas, ed. MIT Committee on the Visual Arts: Cambridge, MA
Sotheby's Photographs from the Collection of Robert Mapplethorpe (auction catalog)
Robert Mapplethorpe: 1970 – 1983. Preface by Sandy Nairne. Institute of Contemporary Art: London.
Robert Mapplethorpe: Flowers. Galerie Watari: Tokyo
Robert Mapplethorpe. Germano Celant, ed. Idea Books Edizioni: Milano
Black Flowers. Fernando Vijande, ed. Fernando Vijande Editor, S.A. : Madrid
Robert Mapplethorpe. Germano Celant, ed. Idea Books Edizioni: Milano
Robert Mapplethorpe. Obalne Galerije Piran: Slovenia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cover photograph of Mapplethorpe's portrait projected on the facade of the Corcoran Gallery of Art, Wahsington, DC. Issue dedicated to the subject of controversial art in light of Senator Jesse Helms war on "obscene and indecent art".

did.unittitle: Subseries Vb. Books related to Punk Rock, Patti Smith, and the Downtown scene
